steps.title: Explain the early reviews
steps.content: The passage says Keller's 1984 edition was later found to have introduced errors, but it also says few Joyce scholars had expertise in the relevant textual issues surrounding competing editions. Most of the people who did have that expertise worked on the edition with Keller.

steps.title: Apply the expertise gap
steps.content: Because independent experts were scarce, early reviewers were likely to lack the combined expertise needed to identify the edition's textual problems before later scrutiny exposed them. Choices A and C predict negative reviews from expert critics, but the passage suggests such critics were mostly unavailable. Choice D relies on publication geography, which the passage never discusses. The answer is B.
